title: Analysis File Frontmatter
description: YAML frontmatter required at the top of every per-file analysis .md

assumes.description: Invariants this file requires of its upstream dependencies.

from.description: Upstream symbol, module, or environment condition

enforced_by.description: Upstream symbol that enforces it, or 'not visible'

enforces.description: Invariants this file guarantees to its downstream consumers.

top_suspicion.description: One sentence identifying the single most suspicious thing in this file, or 'none — looks correct under visible constraints'.

lock.description: If true, this analysis must NOT be overwritten by `sync`, `add`, or any LLM pass. Set on hand-tuned files. `mhng-repo-mind sync` skips locked files entirely.

source.description: If true, this file is a SOURCE — vendored, generated, or otherwise consumed-but-not-owned. Sources get a lighter analysis, are skipped by `audit`, and are listed in SOURCES.md.

disabled.description: If true (spec v1.5), this analysis is kept in the manifest for retrieval but EXCLUDED from `audit`, `compliance`, `coverage`, `drift`. Use for archived/deprecated code that should remain documented but not actively reasoned about.

meta.description: Free-form key-value pairs (spec v1.5). mhng-repo-mind never reads this directly — it's an extension point for users. Examples: { owner: 'billing-team', pii_severity: 'high', last_security_review: '2026-Q1' }. Queryable via `mhng-repo-mind query --meta key=value`.

first_seen_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this node first appeared in the manifest (spec v1.5). Set on creation, never updated. Combined with `analyzed_at`, gives the age of every documented file. Used for 'show me code that's been around 6+ months without re-audit' queries.

semantic_parents.description: Source paths this file conceptually depends on BEYOND import edges (spec v1.5). For example, an `assumes` block points at another file's `enforces` — that's a semantic edge even without an import statement. Surfaces in `drift` and `audit`.

config_parents.description: Source paths whose configuration affects this file (spec v1.5). For example, a Next.js page is configured by `next.config.js` even though it doesn't import it. Lets `drift` flag config-driven impact.

access.description: Access modifier (spec v1.6). `public` = exported across groups, `protected` = usable within the same group only, `private` = internal-only. `audit` flags any cross-group import of a `private` node.

version.description: Version number for this node (spec v1.6). Used for breaking-change governance — bumping the version is required when an `enforces` block is removed or weakened. Refs without a version pin resolve to `latest_version`.

latest_version.description: The maximum version number across all known versions of this node (spec v1.6). Computed by the manifest builder, not set manually.

deprecation_date.description: ISO 8601 date after which this node is considered deprecated (spec v1.6). `mhng-repo-mind deprecated` lists nodes past this date. `audit` flags new dependencies on deprecated nodes.

deprecation_reason.description: Optional one-line explanation of why this node is being deprecated and what to use instead.

generated_by.description: Provenance — which tool/model/version produced this file. Required on every machine-generated analysis. Used by `mhng-repo-mind provenance` and `mhng-repo-mind migrate` to track and re-run analyses across model versions.

tool.description: Always 'mhng-repo-mind' (other tools that produce mhng-repo-mind-shaped output use their own name).

tool_version.description: Semver of the mhng-repo-mind CLI that wrote this file.

spec_version.description: Spec version this file conforms to (matches top-level spec_version).

runner.description: How the model was invoked, e.g. 'claude-code', 'anthropic-sdk', 'openai-sdk'.

runner_version.description: Version of the runner tool, if known.

prompt_path.description: Relative path to the prompt file used (e.g. 'prompts/file-analysis.md').

prompt_sha.description: First 16 hex chars of sha256 of the prompt file at generation time.

settings.description: Inference settings applied (only what's known — leave fields out if not set).

processes.description: Process membership (spec v1.7). On a per-file forensic analysis: lists every process+stage this file belongs to, so the file's analysis links back to all of them. On a process stage .md: typically a single entry naming its own process+stage and breadcrumb. Populated by `mhng-repo-mind process`; never invented by per-file analysis prompts.

stage.description: Stage name (leaf or branch) within that process.

breadcrumb.description: Full path from process root to this node, e.g. ['attribution_insurance','intake','validation'].

md_path.description: Path to the stage's OVERVIEW.md or <name>.md, relative to output_dir.

process_node.description: Self-description block on a process stage .md file (spec v1.7). Identifies which process tree this file represents and wires up navigation.

parent.description: Path to parent OVERVIEW.md, or null at the root.

children.description: Paths to child OVERVIEW.md / leaf .md files (branches only).

intents.description: Structured classification of WHY this file (or a block in it) exists. IDs must come from schemas/intents.v1.yaml. Compliance intents require an `evidence` string and at least one `controls` entry.

locations.description: Line ranges like 'L42-L58' or symbol names for block-scoped tags

evidence.description: One-sentence justification quoting the code or citing a contract. REQUIRED for compliance tags.

controls.description: Specific control IDs within the framework (e.g. '164.312(a)(1)', 'art-17', 'CC6.1'). REQUIRED for compliance tags.
